report_name: Maine Accounting Firm Data Breach Exposes Sensitive Tax and Financial Data of 928 Clients

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Attacker
threat_actors.description: An unnamed attacker responsible for a phishing campaign against Edwards, Faust & Smith, CPAs, which resulted in the compromise of a company computer, a remote server, and the sensitive data of 928 clients.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The initial intrusion vector was a phishing email disguised as a prospective client inquiry, which led to the compromise of a company computer and a remote server.

attack_patterns.name: File and Directory Discovery
attack_patterns.description: After gaining access, the attacker had nearly three months to discover where sensitive client data was stored on the compromised systems.

identities.name: Edwards, Faust & Smith, CPAs
identities.description: A certified public accounting (CPA) firm in Bangor, Maine, that experienced a data breach affecting 928 clients. The breach resulted from a phishing attack and exposed sensitive data including Social Security numbers, tax return information, and financial account details.
